title: Potentilla gilanica
taxonomy: Potentilla gilanica is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family, commonly known as the rose family, is a large and diverse group of plants. Potentilla gilanica is also a member of the genus Potentilla, which includes around 300 species of flowering plants. The genus Potentilla is further classified within the tribe Potentilleae, which is a subtribe of the subfamily Rosoideae. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Potentilla gilanica is closely related to other flowering plants such as roses and apples.
uses: Potentilla gilanica, a plant species native to the Caucasus region, has been found to possess medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. intro: Potentilla gilanica, also known as Iranian potentilla, is a flowering plant species in the family Rosaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating conditions such as wounds, gastrointestinal disorders, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Potentilla gilanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a common ingredient in traditional medicine and dietary supplements.
